title: dioscorea convolvulacea subsp. esurientium
intro: Dioscorea convolvulacea subsp. esurientium, commonly known as the Mexican wild yam, is a species of flowering plant in the yam family, Dioscoreaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in its roots, which contain diosgenin, a steroid saponin used as a precursor to produce progesterone and other corticosteroids. The health benefits of Dioscorea convolvulacea subsp. esurientium include its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate menstrual cramps, menopausal symptoms, and other conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, capsules, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
